root_text.an3.137:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, yāni kānici tantāvutānaṁ vatthānaṁ, kesakambalo tesaṁ paṭikiṭṭho akkhāyati.
root_text.an3.137:1.2: Kesakambalo, bhikkhave, sīte sīto, uṇhe uṇho, dubbaṇṇo, duggandho, dukkhasamphasso.
root_text.an3.137:1.3: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yāni kānici puthusamaṇabrāhmaṇavādānaṁ makkhalivādo tesaṁ paṭikiṭṭho akkhāyati.
root_text.an3.137:2.3: Yepi te, bhikkhave, ahesuṁ atītamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā, tepi bhagavanto kammavādā ceva ahesuṁ kiriyavādā ca vīriyavādā ca.
root_text.an3.137:2.6: Yepi te, bhikkhave, bhavissanti anāgatamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā, tepi bhagavanto kammavādā ceva bhavissanti kiriyavādā ca vīriyavādā ca.
root_text.an3.137:2.9: Ahampi, bhikkhave, etarahi arahaṁ sammāsambuddho kammavādo ceva kiriyavādo ca vīriyavādo ca.
root_text.an3.137:3.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, nadīmukhe khippaṁ uḍḍeyya bahūnaṁ macchānaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya anayāya byasanāya;
root_text.an3.137:3.2: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, makkhali moghapuriso manussakhippaṁ maññe loke uppanno bahūnaṁ sattānaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya anayāya byasanāyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.137:1.1: “Mendicants, a hair blanket is said to be the worst kind of woven cloth.
translation_text.an3.137:1.2: It’s cold in the cold, hot in the heat, ugly, smelly, and unpleasant to touch.
translation_text.an3.137:1.3: In the same way, the teaching of the bamboo-staffed ascetic is said to be the worst of all the doctrines of the various ascetics and brahmins.
translation_text.an3.137:2.1: The bamboo-staffed ascetic, that futile man, has this doctrine and view:
translation_text.an3.137:2.2: ‘There is no power in deeds, action, or energy.’
translation_text.an3.137:2.3: Those who were perfected ones, fully awakened Buddhas in the past also taught the efficacy of deeds, action, and energy.
translation_text.an3.137:2.4: But the bamboo-staffed ascetic opposes them by saying:
translation_text.an3.137:2.5: ‘There is no power in deeds, action, or energy.’
translation_text.an3.137:2.6: Those who will be perfected ones, fully awakened Buddhas in the future will also teach the efficacy of deeds, action, and energy.
translation_text.an3.137:2.7: But the bamboo-staffed ascetic opposes them by saying:
translation_text.an3.137:2.8: ‘There is no power in deeds, action, or energy.’
translation_text.an3.137:2.9: I too, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha in the present, teach the efficacy of deeds, action, and energy.
translation_text.an3.137:2.10: But the bamboo-staffed ascetic opposes me by saying:
translation_text.an3.137:2.11: ‘There is no power in deeds, action, or energy.’
translation_text.an3.137:3.1: It’s like a trap set at the mouth of a river, which would bring harm, suffering, calamity, and disaster for many fish.
translation_text.an3.137:3.2: In the same way that futile man the bamboo-staffed ascetic is a trap for humans, it seems to me. He has come into the world for the harm, suffering, calamity, and disaster of many beings.”

variant_text.an3.137:3.1: khippaṁ → khipaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts1ed) | uḍḍeyya → oḍḍeyya (bj) | bahūnaṁ → bahunnaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
